\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Portfolios are formed in June of each year by independent double-sorts on size (market equity, ME) into 10 deciles and pre-ranking beta into 10 deciles within each size group.
\item Size decile 1 (S1) is smallest; beta decile 1 (B1) is lowest beta.
\item Post-ranking betas are estimated using 24–60 monthly returns after portfolio formation.
\item Market equity is measured in RMB millions.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Portfolios are formed in June of each year on a single characteristic.
\item Left panel: decile 1 (S1) has the smallest market equity.
\item Right panel: decile 1 (B1) has the lowest pre-ranking beta.
\item ME = average market equity in RMB millions.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table III: Fama--MacBeth cross-sectional regressions --- average slopes from monthly regressions of stock returns on beta, size, and book-to-market}
\label{tab:fama_macbeth}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lccccccc}
\toprule
 & (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) & (7) \\
\midrule
Intercept & 0.0132$^{**}$ & 0.0814$^{***}$ & -0.0033 & 0.0825$^{***}$ & -0.0009 & 0.0634$^{**}$ & 0.0671$^{**}$ \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize (2.213)} & {\footnotesize (2.936)} & {\footnotesize (-0.344)} & {\footnotesize (3.075)} & {\footnotesize (-0.098)} & {\footnotesize (2.302)} & {\footnotesize (2.473)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
$\beta$ & -0.0009 &  &  & -0.0008 & -0.0005 &  & -0.0004 \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize (-0.580)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-0.657)} & {\footnotesize (-0.343)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-0.364)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
$\ln(ME)$ &  & -0.0045$^{***}$ &  & -0.0045$^{***}$ &  & -0.0044$^{***}$ & -0.0045$^{***}$ \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-2.946)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-3.021)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-3.035)} & {\footnotesize (-3.137)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
$\ln(BE/ME)$ &  &  & 0.0027$^{***}$ &  & 0.0024$^{**}$ & 0.0027$^{***}$ & 0.0025$^{***}$ \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (2.615)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (2.370)} & {\footnotesize (3.109)} & {\footnotesize (3.005)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Each column reports time-series averages of monthly Fama--MacBeth (1973) cross-sectional regression slopes.
\item Newey--West (1987) $t$-statistics with 12 lags are in parentheses.
\item $^{*}p<0.10$, $^{**}p<0.05$, $^{***}p<0.01$.
\item Returns are measured as raw monthly returns. $\ln(ME)$ is log market equity at June-end; $\ln(BE/ME)$ is log book-to-market measured in December of the prior year.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
